title: 把匿名 Q&A 炼成 95 分 / Hardening an Anonymous Q&A App to 95/100: Supabase Security Deep Dive
summary: 我让 6 个 AI 角色审计我的匿名问答网站，他们在 Postgres 挖出了一个隐藏陷阱：REVOKE SELECT 不只拦 SELECT，还会拦 WHERE。/ 6 AI personas audited my anonymous Q&A site and found a Postgres trap: REVOKE SELECT blocks WHERE predicates too.
body:
# 把匿名 Q&A 网站炼成 95 分：Supabase 安全加固全记录

## 目标：服务 1K 用户，需要 95 分的信心

[AnonCafe](https://anoncafe.life) 是一个匿名问答平台。你可以提问，可以认领别人的问题去回答，所有操作不需要注册。帖子用一个 8 位 access code 来标识所有权——没有账号体系，只有那串字母。

功能上线了几周。但「感觉跑通」和「可以给 1K 个真实用户用」之间，有一道坎。

## 六个 AI 角色，一次网站体检

`/opc loop` 派出六个角色：PM（user journey 断点）、Designer（移动端适配）、New User（靠直觉行动）、Active User（有历史期望）、Security Reviewer（攻击面）、Compliance Reviewer（PII 泄露、删除权）。

每个角色独立输出评估，汇总到科学置信度分数：

```
confidence = ∏ P(dim_i) × 100
```

七个维度：安全性、可靠性、性能、数据完整性、可观测性、内容审核、部署保真度。每个维度上限 99.5%。

**第一轮结果：60.8 分。**

## 那个让我愣了五秒的错误

Security Reviewer 发现：

> `REVOKE SELECT (access_code) ON posts FROM anon` 不只阻止了 anon 查看 access_code 列——**它还阻止了 anon 在 WHERE 子句里引用这个列。**

Postgres 的权限模型：WHERE 谓词里引用一个列，同样需要该列的 SELECT 权限。PostgREST 忠实执行了这个规则。

三条核心路径全静默断了：`getPostByAccessCode()`、`generateAccessCode()` 唯一性检查、`incrementViewCount()`。用户提交帖子后拿到 access code，但用这个 code 找不到帖子。核心 loop 坏掉了，没有任何报错。

## 修法：SECURITY DEFINER 函数

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_post_by_access_code(p_access_code text)
RETURNS jsonb
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER  -- 以表的 owner 权限运行，而不是调用者权限
STABLE
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
  -- WHERE access_code = ... 在这里以 owner 权限执行
  -- REVOKE 对直接查询仍然有效
  RETURN (SELECT jsonb_build_object('id', id, 'content', content, ...) FROM posts
          WHERE access_code = upper(trim(p_access_code)) AND deleted_at IS NULL);
END;
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION get_post_by_access_code(text) TO anon, authenticated;
```

调用者只有 EXECUTE 权限。REVOKE 仍然阻断直接 SELECT，但这三个受控路径恢复了合法操作。

**中途踩坑**：`CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION` 不能改返回类型，报 `ERROR: 42P13`。它发生在包含三个 CREATE FUNCTION 的迁移文件的第三条语句，Postgres 事务回滚把前两个也一起撤销了——没有任何报错。教训：任何替换函数的迁移都先加 `DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS`。

## Admin Dashboard

合规角色指出：没有审核能力的匿名平台不应该上线。三个要求：

1. 不能用 localStorage 伪造 session（原来的实现就是这样）
2. 只有手动创建的账号才能登录
3. 可以看到所有帖子，软删除和还原

**认证**：改用 `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`。**关闭公开注册**的 toggle 在 Authentication → **Settings**，不是 Sign In / Auth Providers 页面（那个是控制认证方式，不是注册权限）——我把两个混淆了。

## 错误收集 + Soft Delete

`app_errors` 表：anon 只能 INSERT，authenticated 才能 SELECT。`window.onerror` 和 `ErrorBoundary` 都 fire-and-forget 到这里。

`deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ` 列：`deletePost()` 设置它，`restorePost()` 清空它，`hardDeletePost()` 真正删除。公开查询加 `.is('deleted_at', null)`。Admin dashboard 帖子管理变成三个 section：Unsolved / Solved / Deleted。

新列需要手动补 `GRANT SELECT (deleted_at) ON posts TO anon, authenticated`——column-level grant 之后新列不自动继承 SELECT 权限。

## 结果：89.9 分

部署保真度 0.80 → 0.97，可观测性 0.85 → 0.95，内容审核 0.82 → 0.93。距离 95 分还差 5 分——剩下的 gap 是「人工验证证据」，这是代码替代不了的。

不同角色关注不同角落——安全审计员不关心字体大小，设计师不关心 WHERE 谓词权限。让每个角色只做它擅长的事，漏洞会少很多。

---

# Hardening an Anonymous Q&A App to 95/100: A Supabase Security Deep Dive

## The Goal

[AnonCafe](https://anoncafe.life) is an anonymous Q&A platform. Post a question, someone claims and answers it, no account required. Each post gets an 8-character access code proving ownership.

After a few weeks of the core loop working, I needed to bridge the gap from "feels like it works" to "ready for 1K real users." The answer: a serious audit.

## Six AI Personas, One Site Audit

`/opc loop` dispatched six roles: PM (journey breakpoints), Designer (mobile layout), New User (instinct-driven), Active User (has prior expectations), Security Reviewer (attack surface), Compliance Reviewer (PII, deletion rights).

Scientific confidence score: `confidence = ∏ P(dim_i) × 100` across 7 dimensions, each capped at 99.5%.

**Round 1: 60.8/100.**

## The Mistake That Made Me Read It Twice

The Security Reviewer flagged:

> `REVOKE SELECT (access_code) ON posts FROM anon` doesn't just prevent anon from *seeing* the column — **it also prevents anon from referencing it in a WHERE clause.**

Postgres: referencing a column in a WHERE predicate requires SELECT permission on that column, same as putting it in a SELECT list. PostgREST enforces this faithfully.

Three core paths silently broke: `getPostByAccessCode()`, uniqueness check in `generateAccessCode()`, `incrementViewCount()`. Users could submit posts and receive an access code, then fail to retrieve their own post. The core loop was broken, invisibly.

## The Fix: SECURITY DEFINER Functions

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_post_by_access_code(p_access_code text)
RETURNS jsonb LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER STABLE SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN (SELECT jsonb_build_object('id', id, 'content', content, ...)
          FROM posts
          WHERE access_code = upper(trim(p_access_code)) AND deleted_at IS NULL);
END;
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION get_post_by_access_code(text) TO anon, authenticated;
```

Callers have EXECUTE permission only. The REVOKE still blocks direct SELECT. Three controlled bypass paths restore legitimate operations.

**Trap**: `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION` can't change return type — `ERROR: 42P13`. It happened as the third statement in a 3-function migration file, causing Postgres to silently rollback all three. Rule: always start with `DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS` for any function being replaced.

## Admin Dashboard

Compliance raised: an anonymous platform with no moderation capability shouldn't go live.

**Auth**: switched to `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` — replacing the old `isAdmin: true` in localStorage. **Disable public signup**: the toggle is in Authentication → **Settings**, NOT the Sign In / Auth Providers page (that controls *how* people auth, not *whether* they can register — I confused the two).

## Error Collection + Soft Delete

`app_errors` table: anon can INSERT, only authenticated can SELECT. `window.onerror` and `ErrorBoundary` fire-and-forget into it.

`deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ`: `deletePost()` sets it, `restorePost()` clears it, `hardDeletePost()` truly removes. Public queries add `.is('deleted_at', null)`. Admin gets three sections: Unsolved / Solved / Deleted.

New column requires explicit `GRANT SELECT (deleted_at) ON posts TO anon, authenticated` — after column-level REVOKE, new columns don't auto-inherit SELECT.

## Result: 89.9/100

Deployment Fidelity 0.80→0.97, Observability 0.85→0.95, Moderation 0.82→0.93. Remaining 5-point gap: manual verification evidence that code can't replace.

Different roles care about different corners. Security won't notice font size. A designer won't think about WHERE predicate permissions. Letting each role do only what it's good at leaves fewer gaps.
